channel.title: 2026-07 on Sujith Quintelier
channel.description: Recent content in 2026-07 on Sujith Quintelier

item.title: github: Copilot cloud agent for Linear is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub says Copilot cloud agent is now generally available for Linear. Users can assign Linear issues to the agent, which then analyzes the issue contents and works asynchronously in the background.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub MCP Server supports the next MCP specification
item.description:
GitHub MCP Server will support the upcoming MCP spec ahead of its official release. The key protocol change is a move to a stateless core on 28 July 2026.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix failing Actions checks with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you ask Copilot coding agent to investigate and fix failing GitHub Actions checks on a pull request directly from the mobile app. The feature starts from a failed check and uses Copilot cloud agent to work on the issue.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Multi-select fields for Projects and Issues in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Projects and Issue fields now support multi-select fields in public preview, allowing a single field to store multiple values. This lets you tag an item with all relevant labels or categories instead of choosing just one.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure DDoS Protection custom policy
item.description:
Azure DDoS Protection custom policy is in public preview. It lets customers set per-resource DDoS mitigation thresholds for protected Standard Load Balancer frontend IP configurations.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Agent automation controls in GitHub Issues in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Issues now exposes the reason behind agent-driven changes and lets users review proposed automation actions before they are applied. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Email threat landscape: Q2 2026 trends and insights
item.description:
Microsoft reports that Q2 2026 phishing activity declined in several major techniques after its disruption of the Tycoon2FA platform, but attackers shifted toward Teams-based social engineering and more automated, multi-stage attack chains.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Standard service endpoint
item.description:
Standard service endpoint is in public preview. It provides a more scalable and secure way to connect IaaS workloads to Azure PaaS services within the Private Link family, addressing scale and management limits of earlier approaches.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: New Copilot usage metrics impact dashboard
item.description:
GitHub released a new Copilot metrics impact dashboard for enterprise administrators and organization owners. It is meant to help them report Copilot impact using usage metrics and related data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Real world incident response: Microsoft and AXA XL strengthen cyber resilience
item.description:
Microsoft and AXA XL are collaborating to give cyber insurance policyholders direct access to Microsoft Incident Response services. The goal is to help organizations coordinate technical response, business decisions, and insurance actions during incidents.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Upcoming GHES change impacting uploading support bundles
item.description:
GitHub will begin rejecting some GHES support bundle uploads starting August 18, 2026 because of an upcoming security change. Administrators should review the new upload requirements before that date to avoid failed submissions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: Building Agents that Act on Your Behalf with Toolboxes in Foundry
item.description:
Toolboxes in Microsoft Foundry are presented as a way to let agents act on a user’s behalf while handling delegated identity across tools and services. The post highlights using a private Entra-protected MCP server alongside Microsoft’s managed Work IQ MCP server in the same agent flow.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: github: Gemini 3.6 Flash is now available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out Gemini 3.6 Flash, Google’s latest Flash model. GitHub says it is intended for web and app development, coding, and longer-horizon agentic tasks, with configurable behavior.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: AI credit pools for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Billing UI now lets you manage a cost center’s AI credit pool directly when creating or editing cost centers. Before this change, that configuration was only available through another interface.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot users can now see AI credits used per billing cycle
item.description:
Copilot Business and Copilot Enterprise users can now view how many AI credits they have used in the current billing cycle, even if they do not have an individual budget. The usage count is available in GitHub Copilot usage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Protect sensitive generative AI telemetry in Application Insights and Microsoft Foundry
item.description:
Application Insights now stores generative AI content in a dedicated GenAIContent table (AppGenAIContent in Log Analytics), enabling access controls for sensitive AI telemetry. The update is in public preview.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: IPv6 support for Azure VPN Gateway
item.description:
Azure VPN Gateway now supports IPv6 inner traffic in dual-stack VPN tunnels. The feature is available on all production SKU gateways that use standard public IPs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality is now generally available for GitHub Enterprise Cloud and GitHub Team. The announcement says it is intended to help with the growing volume of code produced with AI assistance.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository-level GitHub Copilot usage metrics generally available
item.description:
GitHub Copilot usage metrics REST API now includes repository-level activity. Two new endpoints provide a daily per-repository breakdown of pull request activity for Copilot coding agent and Copilot code review.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app now available in the usage metrics API
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot usage metrics API now includes GitHub Copilot app usage in enterprise and organization 1-day and 28-day reports. This adds admin visibility into Copilot app usage data.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot code review: Customization and configurability improvements
item.description:
Copilot code review now supports a firewall, custom setup steps, and independent runner configurations. It also reads custom instructions from the head branch to make testing and validation easier.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix pull request comments with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you select “Fix with Copilot” from Copilot code review pull request comments. The action is available in the pull request’s main view and uses the Copilot cloud agent.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Functions support for Python 3.14
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports Python 3.14 for local development and deployment to Linux-based Azure Functions plans. This lets apps move to Python 3.14 for security, longer support, and continued compatibility.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Functions Support for PowerShell 7.6
item.description:
Azure Functions now supports PowerShell 7.6 in public preview. You can develop locally with PowerShell 7.6 and deploy to Azure Functions plans.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Microsoft at Black Hat USA 2026: Defending trust in the age of AI and supply chain attacks
item.description:
Microsoft Security says it will be at Black Hat USA 2026 with sessions on supply chain research, hands-on security experiences, expert conversations, and a reception. The post frames the event around trust, AI, and supply chain attacks.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Defender security assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server
item.description:
Microsoft Defender CSPM assessments for Azure Database for PostgreSQL Flexible Server are now generally available. The assessments can be used to continuously evaluate the security posture of PostgreSQL flexible servers in Azure.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Encryption in Transit for Azure Files NFS Shares in Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS)
item.description:
Azure Kubernetes Service (AKS) now generally supports Encryption in Transit for Azure Files NFS v4.1 volumes through the Azure File CSI driver. When enabled, data transferred between AKS workloads and Azure Files NFS shares is encrypted in transit.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: ACR Stealer: Two observed intrusion chains amid increased threat activity
item.description:
Microsoft Defender Experts observed increased ACR Stealer activity from late April to mid-June 2026 across customer environments. The campaigns used ClickFix lures to steal browser credentials, authentication tokens, and sensitive documents from enterprise environments.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Advanced search for Projects is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Projects views now support advanced search in the filter bar. You can combine conditions with logical AND and OR to build more precise views without maintaining a separate setup.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Repository admins can archive pull requests
item.description:
Repository admins can archive pull requests to remove them from public view without deleting them. Archived pull requests are closed and locked.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: REST API endpoints for Visual Studio Subscription management
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud admins can now use REST API endpoints to manage Visual Studio Subscription (VSS) assignments programmatically. The new endpoint returns all VSS assignments for an enterprise, including assignment details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Xcode 27 runner image now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub-hosted macOS runners now support Xcode 27 in public preview, letting you build and test Apple apps against the latest Xcode version. This is an early-access runner image release.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: Announcing .NET Modernization for Beginners
item.description:
Microsoft announced a free, open-source hands-on course for modernizing a legacy ASP.NET application to .NET 10 using the GitHub Copilot modernization agent. The course is step-by-step and aimed at beginners.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: security: Least privilege for AI agents: Identity, access, and tool binding
item.description:
Microsoft Security published a post on applying least-privilege principles to AI agents, focusing on identity, access control, tool binding, and auditing as agents become more autonomous.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: security: Unpacking the AsyncAPI npm supply chain compromise and import-time payload delivery
item.description:
Microsoft reports that attackers compromised AsyncAPI npm packages and used trusted CI/CD workflows to distribute malware via npm. The writeup details the attack chain, import-time payload delivery, and suggested defenses.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Improvements to secret scanning and public monitoring
item.description:
GitHub rolled out updates to secret scanning and public monitoring. Resend is now a secret scanning partner, and secret scanning can detect new secret types from APIclub.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Announcing: GitHub Copilot and Claude Code connectors in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes generally available connectors for GitHub Copilot and Claude Code. These connectors let .NET and Python agents delegate coding tasks to either system without custom adapter code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Multi-agent orchestration patterns including Magentic in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework is now generally available for multi-agent orchestration patterns, including the dynamic Magentic pattern. The announcement cites benchmark results for Magentic, including 38% on GAIA and competitive results on AssistantBench and WebArena.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Agent Harness in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now has general availability for Agent Harness, the production runtime for agents built with the framework. Preview harness users can move the same agent code into production with backward compatibility.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Multi-agent orchestration SDK with the Microsoft Foundry Agent Framework in C# and Python
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry has introduced Agent Framework, a unified multi-agent orchestration SDK for C# and Python. It consolidates orchestration patterns previously split across AutoGen and Semantic Kernel into a single set of abstractions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Tracing for Microsoft Agent Framework (Python and .NET)
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry now provides public preview tracing support for Microsoft Agent Framework in Python and .NET. The feature lets developers observe agent reasoning, tool calls, and model interactions during execution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: CodeAct pattern and Hyperlight containers in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes the CodeAct pattern in public preview. It lets agents execute multi-step plans as a single code block instead of sequential tool calls, which reduced end-to-end latency in representative workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Episodic procedural memory in Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now has episodic procedural memory in public preview. It stores successful task patterns so agents can reuse past action sequences, tool choices, and related context on similar future requests.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: Agent Channel for Microsoft Agent Framework multi-agent communication
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes Agent Channel in public preview for multi-agent communication. It provides a built-in way for agents to exchange typed messages using request/response or fire-and-forget patterns without custom routing code.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure SQL updates for mid-July 2026
item.description:
Azure SQL added Shortcut Configuration in Visual Studio Code preview, allowing users to customize keyboard shortcuts for Quick Queries, the Results Grid, and the Query Editor. The update is listed as a public preview for mid-July 2026.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Expanding Azure Arc SQL Migration with SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines
item.description:
Azure Arc SQL migration now supports SQL Server on Azure Virtual Machines as a target. Arc-enabled SQL Server instances can be migrated to either Azure SQL Managed Instance or SQL Server on Azure infrastructure using the same migration solution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Turning threat intelligence into decisive action with Defender Experts
item.description:
Microsoft Security describes a Defender Experts post about helping security teams turn threat intelligence into action across endpoints, identities, cloud workloads, and third-party tools. The excerpt emphasizes that teams have more visibility but still face uncertainty from fragmented signals.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Advanced platform metrics in Azure Monitor
item.description:
Azure Monitor now has advanced platform metrics in Public Preview starting July 15, 2026. The preview adds improved visibility into platform performance, resource health, and operational trends for supported Azure services.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: Announcing: DevUI Agent Inspector for Microsoft Agent Framework
item.description:
Microsoft Agent Framework now includes DevUI Agent Inspector in public preview. It provides a local debugging view for agent runs with a tree of model calls, tool invocations, and intermediate state, plus controls to pause, replay, and edit prompts during execution.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio — June update
item.description:
June 2026’s Visual Studio Copilot update focuses on usage visibility, a new trust layer for MCP servers, and the first C++ scenarios. The post emphasizes controls and clarity rather than new broad functionality.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot for JetBrains expands BYOK capabilities
item.description:
GitHub Copilot for JetBrains now supports expanded bring-your-own-key (BYOK) customization across all Copilot tiers, with broader model provider flexibility and improved plugin/provider experiences. The update also includes changes to conversational interactions in the IDE.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Front Door edge actions
item.description:
Azure Front Door edge actions are now in public preview. They let customers run lightweight JavaScript during request processing at the edge for real-time decisions closer to users.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Code scanning shows AI security detections on pull requests
item.description:
GitHub code scanning now shows AI-powered security detections on pull requests. The feature expands coverage to languages and frameworks not currently supported by CodeQL.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: dotnet: .NET and .NET Framework July 2026 servicing releases updates
item.description:
The post is a brief recap of July 2026 servicing updates for .NET and .NET Framework. The provided content does not include version numbers, fixes, or platform-specific details.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: dotnet: CoreCLR Progress and the Mono Timeline for .NET MAUI
item.description:
.NET MAUI mobile apps now use CoreCLR as the only runtime in .NET 11 Preview 6. The post says the team is at this transition point and asks developers to test the change now.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: k8s: Building a Custom Metrics Exporter for Kubernetes
item.description:
The post explains how to build a custom Prometheus metrics exporter for Kubernetes in Go, package it as a minimal container image, and expose it for scraping by Prometheus. It also shows how to verify the target in Prometheus and notes that a metrics adapter such as Prometheus Adapter is needed if you want to use the exported metrics for HorizontalPodAutoscaler scaling.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: dotnet: .NET 11 Preview 6 is now available!
item.description:
The post announces .NET 11 Preview 6 and says it includes updates across runtime, SDK, libraries, ASP.NET Core, .NET MAUI, C#, Entity Framework Core, F#, and container images. No specific feature details are included in the provided text.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: Dependabot version updates introduce default package cooldown
item.description:
Dependabot version updates now use a default package cooldown: it will wait at least three days after a new release appears in the registry before opening a version update pull request. This applies to version updates and delays PR creation for recently published releases.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Security reviews now available in the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
GitHub Copilot app now supports running security reviews on in-flight code changes with the /security-review slash command. The feature is available in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Manage secret scanning custom patterns via REST API
item.description:
GitHub now supports managing secret scanning custom patterns through the REST API. The new generally available endpoints let security teams list, create, and edit custom patterns programmatically.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Code Quality license estimate in public preview
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now shows the number of active committers for repositories across an enterprise, providing an estimate of Code Quality license cost before it is finalized. The feature is in public preview.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Defending SaaS-based applications against ShinyHunters OAuth abuse
item.description:
Microsoft Threat Intelligence reported ShinyHunters-linked activity targeting SaaS-based applications through OAuth abuse, vishing, supply-chain compromise, and misconfigured guest access. The post focuses on how these techniques were used against SaaS environments and how to defend against them.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: k8s: Operating AI/ML Workloads on Kubernetes: A Headlamp Plugin for Kubeflow
item.description:
Kubernetes announced a Headlamp plugin for Kubeflow that surfaces Kubeflow CRDs and related Pod-level state directly in the Headlamp Kubernetes UI. The plugin is aimed at operators and SREs troubleshooting ML workloads and works with modular Kubeflow installs by discovering available API groups at runtime.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: k8s: Kubernetes Dashboard to Headlamp: A Step-by-Step Guide
item.description:
Kubernetes Dashboard users can migrate to Headlamp by choosing either a desktop or in-cluster deployment. The guide covers kubeconfig-based auth, OIDC for shared installs, multi-cluster support, YAML-based resource creation, debugging features, and cleanup of Dashboard-specific access and RBAC.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Databricks SQL Serverless in UK West
item.description:
Azure Databricks SQL Serverless is now generally available in the UK West region starting July 13, 2026. Eligible UK West workspaces can use it for analytics workloads with instant compute, automated scaling, and less infrastructure management.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: security: Microsoft Entra ID security updates: Passkeys are the default authentication method in Entra ID
item.description:
Microsoft Entra ID now uses passkeys as the default sign-in method and introduces a new authentication model for SMS and voice. The post also points users to preparation guidance for the change.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: A wind farm you can talk to: Building a real-time 3D digital twin on Microsoft Fabric
item.description:
The article describes building a real-time 3D digital twin for a wind farm on Microsoft Fabric by combining geometry, telemetry, and language in one simulation. It emphasizes using the model to spend more time on architectural decisions.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: Separate SSO and Organizations pages in Settings
item.description:
GitHub split the combined SSO & Organizations page in user settings into two separate pages: SSO and Organizations. The change is intended to make these settings easier to find and manage.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: Retirement: Support for Python-2.7; 3.8 and PowerShell- 7.1; 7.2 will be retired on September 30, 2026
item.description:
Azure Automation will stop supporting Python 2.7, Python 3.8, and PowerShell 7.1/7.2 starting October 1, 2026. Runbooks may still run, but these runtimes will no longer receive security updates or bug fixes.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: CodeQL 2.26.0 adds Kotlin 2.4.0 support and AI prompt injection detection
item.description:
CodeQL 2.26.0 adds support for Kotlin 2.4.0 and introduces detection for AI prompt injection issues in code scanning. The release updates the static analysis engine used by GitHub code scanning.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Clearer names for secret scanning detector types
item.description:
GitHub is renaming secret scanning detector types to make it clearer how each detector finds secrets. This is a naming-only change.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Securing our future: July 2026 progress report on Microsoft’s Secure Future Initiative
item.description:
Microsoft published its July 2026 Secure Future Initiative progress report, summarizing work on secure foundations, AI-assisted defense, and broader cybersecurity readiness. The provided text is only a brief overview and does not include specific implementation details or metrics.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Per-user states for multi-user budgets in the REST API
item.description:
GitHub REST API now returns per-user progress for multi-user budgets from a single endpoint. This lets clients identify users nearing their limits without making separate requests.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Improved filters and sorting for Copilot sessions
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports improved filtering and sorting for Copilot sessions. The update is meant to make it easier to find specific sessions as the list grows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Manage Azure Chaos Studio from the Azure CLI
item.description:
Azure Chaos Studio now has a public preview Azure CLI extension (az chaos) for creating and running resilience scenarios. It reduces the need to manually craft REST calls and JSON files, and includes a one-step setup command to provision the required workspace and related resources.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: New pull requests dashboard is now generally available
item.description:
GitHub’s refreshed pull requests dashboard is now generally available at github.com/pulls. It provides a single place to track, prioritize, and act on pull requests that need attention.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Open AI GPT-5.6 on Azure Databricks
item.description:
OpenAI GPT-5.6 is now generally available on Azure Databricks. Customers can use GPT 5.6 purchased through Microsoft Foundry via a Model Serving Endpoint in Azure Databricks.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: dotnet: Modernize .NET applications in the GitHub Copilot app
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot app now includes an interactive upgrade canvas for modernizing .NET applications. It supports the upgrade process from assessment through execution.

  • Source: .NET

item.title: github: OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 Sol, Terra, and Luna are now available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
GitHub Copilot is rolling out OpenAI’s GPT-5.6 family. The release adds three variants — Sol, Terra, and Luna — so users can choose a model based on the task.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Organization-level targeting for GitHub Code Quality
item.description:
GitHub Code Quality now supports organization-level targeting, letting owners enable or disable it for only selected repositories instead of the entire organization. This adds finer-grained control over rollout and removal.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: GigaWiper: Anatomy of a destructive backdoor assembled from multiple malware
item.description:
Microsoft describes GigaWiper as a destructive backdoor that merges wiping and ransomware-like capabilities into one platform. The analysis focuses on how it reuses code from multiple malware families and offers guidance for detection and defense.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Ask Copilot for a repository overview
item.description:
GitHub Copilot can now provide a high-level overview of a repository from the repository home page. The feature is meant to help users quickly understand repositories they are exploring for the first time.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Innersource security advisories are generally available
item.description:
GitHub Advanced Security enterprise customers can now publish internal security advisories for innersource repositories. These advisories work like open source advisories, but visibility is restricted to repositories owned by the enterprise.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise-managed OpenTelemetry export for VS Code and CLI
item.description:
GitHub Copilot now supports enterprise-managed OpenTelemetry export for VS Code and CLI, letting organizations direct telemetry to an approved collector centrally instead of requiring developers to set OTEL_* environment variables. The configuration is delivered through enterprise-managed settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Deploy managed Copilot settings via MDM in VS Code and CLI
item.description:
Enterprise admins can now push managed GitHub Copilot settings to devices using native MDM or file-based configuration, in addition to the existing server-managed channel. The update applies to VS Code and the CLI.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot in Visual Studio Code, June 2026 releases
item.description:
This changelog covers GitHub Copilot updates in Visual Studio Code across VS Code v1.123 through v1.127, released during June and early July 2026. The provided excerpt does not include specific feature or bug-fix details.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: setup-java v5.5.0: signature verification, Kona JDK, and Maven fixes
item.description:
setup-java v5.5.0 adds cryptographic signature verification for downloaded JDKs, support for Kona JDK, and several Maven-related fixes. It is the release over v5.4.0.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Protecting Microsoft at AI speed: How SFI proactively hardens our cloud
item.description:
Microsoft says its Secure Future Initiative (SFI) uses security requirements, threat intelligence, and operational frameworks to define what a well-defended cloud service should look like. It emphasizes continuously evaluating live services against those requirements at AI speed.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: commandline: How my dev machine built 1,111 word-processing features and forgot to add a ruler
item.description:
The piece describes an experiment where a complex agentic AI loop was used to build an online collaborative word processor, reflecting on the results and what emerged from letting the system run mostly on its own. The title suggests the system produced many features but still missed basic editor functionality like a ruler.

  • Source: Microsoft Command Line Blog

item.title: github: npm install-time security and GAT bypass2fa deprecation
item.description:
npm v12 is now generally available and tagged latest. It enables the install-time security defaults announced in June and starts deprecating GAT bypass2fa.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: k8s: Announcing etcd v3.7.0
item.description:
etcd v3.7.0 adds RangeStream for chunked range results, improves range/lease/watch performance, and completes the move off legacy v2store dependencies. It also includes protobuf dependency cleanup, Unix socket support, new metrics, and several upgrade-breaking removals.

  • Source: Kubernetes Official Blog

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Fix merge conflicts with Copilot cloud agent
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now lets you fix pull request merge conflicts using the Copilot cloud agent. This makes it possible to unblock PRs from mobile while away from a desktop.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Mobile: Live notifications for Copilot CLI sessions
item.description:
GitHub Mobile now supports live coding agent notifications for remote Copilot CLI sessions. This lets users follow agent activity that starts outside the app.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Add review cycles and time to adoption phases in the usage API
item.description:
The Copilot usage metrics API adds two review-velocity fields for each AI adoption phase in enterprise and organization reports. The new data extends the adoption phase cohort fields with review cycles and time in phase.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Codex as agent provider and agentic enhancements in JetBrains IDEs
item.description:
GitHub added Codex as a new agent provider in public preview. JetBrains IDE updates also include Hooks support in the Customizations editor, richer MCP server management, and custom model support via configuration.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Kimi K2.7 now available for Copilot Business and Enterprise
item.description:
Kimi K2.7 is now available on GitHub Copilot Business and Enterprise. This expands availability beyond the Copilot Pro, Pro+, and Max plans announced on July 1, 2026.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Exceptions in WAF for Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door
item.description:
Azure Web Application Firewall now has an exceptions feature in public preview for Azure Application Gateway and Azure Front Door. It lets you allow specific requests that would otherwise be blocked as false positives, complementing existing exclusions.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Azure Chaos Studio Workspaces and Scenarios
item.description:
Azure Chaos Studio introduces public preview support for Workspaces and Scenarios. Workspaces let you target an application’s scope, such as a subscription, resource group, or service group, to run chaos testing in a more application-centric way.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Export historical data from Log Analytics workspace with Export jobs
item.description:
Azure Log Analytics now has a public preview Export job feature for exporting historical workspace data to an Azure Storage account. Exports are driven by a query and time range so you can retrieve only the needed data for external use.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Network Security Perimeter support for Azure Event Hubs
item.description:
Azure Event Hubs now generally supports Network Security Perimeter (NSP). This lets organizations define a logical network isolation boundary for PaaS resources and control public network access using perimeter-based access rules.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Confidential Computing support for Azure Event Hubs Dedicated
item.description:
Azure Event Hubs Dedicated now supports Confidential Computing. It uses hardware-based trusted execution environments to help protect streaming data while it is processed in memory.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Azure Red Hat OpenShift in Chile Central
item.description:
Azure Red Hat OpenShift is now generally available in Azure Chile Central. This adds support for OpenShift deployments in Microsoft’s first Azure region in Chile and expands availability in South America.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Per-user budgets for cost centers in the billing UI
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports creating per-user budgets for cost centers directly in the billing UI. Enterprise admins can manage these user-level budgets alongside cost centers and budgets in one place.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning extended metadata and multipart validation
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now surfaces extended metadata for supported secret types to help identify ownership and impact of leaked secrets. The update also includes general availability of extended metadata checks and multipart validation support.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Restrict who can dismiss reviews in rulesets
item.description:
GitHub repository rulesets now support restricting who can dismiss pull request reviews. The feature is generally available and lets you control which users can clear review requirements.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Copilot app available to all
item.description:
The GitHub Copilot app is now available on all Copilot plans. Users can sign in with a GitHub account and use it for agent-driven development from desktop on macOS, Windows, and Linux.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot Billing Preview app will be retired on August 3
item.description:
GitHub will retire the Copilot Billing Preview app on August 3, 2026. Users who rely on it for Copilot spend review are directed to use built-in GitHub visibility instead.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: ai: What’s New in Microsoft Foundry | June 2026
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry’s June 2026 update adds Claude GA and several agent-related features released between Build 2026 and the end of June. It also calls out autopilot agents, expanded Toolboxes and Routines, and Agent Optimizer entering private preview.

  • Source: Microsoft AI Foundry Blog

item.title: security: 5 insights from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar™ for Cloud Security Posture Management
item.description:
Microsoft summarizes five takeaways from Frost & Sullivan’s 2025 Frost Radar for Cloud Security Posture Management, highlighting a shift from point-in-time compliance to continuous risk management. The post frames CSPM as evolving toward broader, ongoing cloud security oversight.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP
item.description:
Microsoft Entra ID-based access for Azure Blob Storage SFTP is now generally available in all regions. It allows Entra ID identities, including guest users via Entra External Identities, to connect to Azure Blob Storage over SFTP.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Support 5x churn in Azure Site Recovery
item.description:
Azure Site Recovery now supports up to 5x churn, or 500 MB/s per VM. This increases disaster recovery support for high-IOPS workloads.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Improved accuracy and coverage in Copilot usage metrics reports
item.description:
GitHub improved the Copilot usage metrics API to make reports more complete and accurate. The update adds support for Copilot CLI suggested lines of code and includes users that were previously only partially captured.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Upcoming deprecation of Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash
item.description:
GitHub will deprecate Gemini 2.5 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash across all Copilot experiences on July 31. This includes Copilot Chat, inline edits, ask and agent modes, and code completions.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally available: Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 on Azure Databricks
item.description:
Azure Databricks now supports Anthropic Claude Sonnet 5 via AI Model Serving. The update says the model offers near-Opus-level intelligence with Sonnet-level cost efficiency and speed.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Copilot CLI no longer needs a personal access token in GitHub Actions
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI can now run in GitHub Actions with the built-in GITHUB_TOKEN. A personal access token is no longer required for this workflow.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot agent session streaming is now in public preview
item.description:
GitHub has put Copilot agent session streaming into public preview for Enterprise Cloud customers with enterprise managed users. Session data is available across Copilot clients, including cloud agents on github.com and data-resident deployments.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Cost centers now support AI credit pools
item.description:
Cost centers can now be assigned caps on how much of an enterprise’s monthly included AI credits they can consume. The feature is available through the REST API.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Improving security posture across the Microsoft partner ecosystem
item.description:
Microsoft says it is improving security across its partner ecosystem by tightening CSP vetting, applying least-privilege access, increasing monitoring, and promoting risk-management best practices. The post is framed as guidance for partners rather than a product release.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog

item.title: github: Issue fields are now generally available
item.description:
Issue fields are now generally available for all GitHub organizations on Free, Team, Enterprise, and GitHub Enterprise Cloud with data residency plans. Support will also ship in GitHub Enterprise Server 3.23.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises
item.description:
GitHub announced secret scanning public monitoring for enterprises, aimed at helping organizations detect and respond to secret leaks as soon as they appear publicly. The post is a brief announcement and does not include implementation details in the provided text.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprises can default to auto model selection
item.description:
Enterprise admins can now set model: auto in enterprise-managed managed-settings.json so GitHub Copilot uses auto model selection by default for new conversations. This setting is added in .github-private/.github/copilot/managed-settings.json for enterprise-managed configuration.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: GitHub Models is being fully retired on July 30, 2026
item.description:
GitHub Models will be fully retired on July 30, 2026. GitHub had already announced in June that it was closing the service to new customers.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Enterprise managed-settings.json is generally available
item.description:
GitHub Enterprise Cloud now supports AI standards configuration through a managed-settings.json file stored in a .github-private repository within a selected organization. This gives enterprises a centralized way to define governance and related settings.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Secret scanning adds validators for Asana, IBM, and MessageBird
item.description:
GitHub secret scanning now validates Asana, IBM, and MessageBird secrets to help determine whether detected credentials are still active. The update adds validity checks for these secret types.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: New C++ language server config skill for Copilot CLI
item.description:
The Microsoft C++ Language Server is now available as a Copilot Plugins marketplace plugin. It includes a built-in setup skill for Copilot CLI to automate project setup.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Kimi K2.7 Code is generally available in GitHub Copilot
item.description:
Kimi K2.7 Code is now generally available in GitHub Copilot. It is the first open-weight model available as a selectable option in the Copilot model picker.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Copilot vision is generally available
item.description:
Copilot Vision is now generally available. You can attach images and PDFs to chat prompts so Copilot can use them alongside your code when responding.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: github: Set AI credit session limits in Copilot CLI and SDK
item.description:
GitHub Copilot CLI and the GitHub Copilot SDK now support AI credit session limits, letting you cap how much an agent can spend in a single session. This is intended to help control usage during longer-running agent workflows.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: New Powershell module: Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer
item.description:
Azure announced the generally available Az.PostgreSQLFlexibleServer PowerShell module, a renamed module for managing Azure Database for PostgreSQL with PowerShell. It replaces the previous Az.PostgreSql-related module naming with a more streamlined experience.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Document PII playground sample in Microsoft Foundry NextGen
item.description:
Microsoft Foundry NextGen now includes a public preview playground for document-based PII detection in Azure AI Language. The sample loads a prepared document, runs document PII detection, and displays the redacted output.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Generally Available: Document PII NextGen Playground in Azure AI Language
item.description:
Azure AI Language’s Document PII NextGen Playground is now generally available. It provides a refreshed experience for evaluating document PII detection with curated sample inputs and outputs.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [In preview] Public Preview: Instant Access via application consistent restore points
item.description:
Azure added Instant Access for VM Restore Points in public preview. It lets you restore disks immediately after a restore point is created, without waiting for background replication.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: azure: [Launched] Public Preview: Azure Storage Mover now supports migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS)
item.description:
Azure Storage Mover Public Preview now supports cloud-to-cloud migration from Google Cloud Storage (GCS) to Azure Blob Storage. Migration uses the S3-compatible interface.

  • Source: Azure Updates

item.title: github: Browser tools for GitHub Copilot in VS Code are generally available
item.description:
Browser tools for GitHub Copilot in VS Code are now generally available. GitHub also clarified which permissions remain under user control and noted that existing network domain controls still apply.

  • Source: GitHub

item.title: security: Microsoft named a leader in the Frost Radar for cloud and application runtime security
item.description:
Microsoft says Frost & Sullivan named it a leader in the Frost Radar for cloud and application runtime security. The post frames this as recognition for a unified approach to cloud and application security focused on runtime risk reduction.

  • Source: Microsoft Security Blog
